searchDocs.title: Getting Started
searchDocs.content: Getting Started This will be implemented at a later date
interface SignalConnection { Connected: boolean Disconnect: (SignalConnection) → () }  Returns a constructed Signal object with the proper disconnectFunction.

searchDocs.title: Disconnect​
searchDocs.content: </> SignalConnection:Disconnect() → () Disconnects a SignalConnection from the signal.

searchDocs.title: Signal​
searchDocs.content: </> interface Signal { Connect: ( Signal, (...any) → () ) → (SignalConnection) Destroy: (Signal) → () Fire: ( Signal, ...any ) → () FireAsync: ( Signal, ...any ) → () Once: ( Signal, (...any) → () ) → (SignalConnection) Wait: (Signal) → (...any) }

searchDocs.title: new​
searchDocs.content: </> Signal.new() → Signal Returns a constructed Signal object

searchDocs.title: Destroy​
searchDocs.content: </> Signal:Destroy() → () Cleans up Signal object by disconnecting all connections. signal:Destroy()

searchDocs.title: Connect​
searchDocs.content: </> Signal:Connect(callback: (...any) → ()) → SignalConnection Connects a given function to the Signal object. Will run until :Disconnect() is called, or the connection's signal is destroyed. signal:Connect(function(msg: string) print(msg) end) signal:Fire("Message 1")

searchDocs.title: Once​
searchDocs.content: </> Signal:Once(callback: (...any) → ()) → SignalConnection Connects a given function to the Signal object. Connection remains open until it is first fired, then cleans itself up. signal:Once(function(msg: string) print(msg) end) signal:Fire("Message 1") signal:Fire("Message 1") -- Will not print

searchDocs.title: Wait​
searchDocs.content: This is a yielding function. When called, it will pause the Lua thread that called the function until a result is ready to be returned, without interrupting other scripts. Yields </> Signal:Wait() → ...any Yields the current thread until the event is fired, returning the results and resuming the thread. task.spawn(function() local msg = signal:Wait() print(msg) end) task.wait(3) signal:Fire("Hello, world!") -- Prints output 3 seconds after script is executed

searchDocs.title: Fire​
searchDocs.content: </> Signal:Fire(...: any) → () Fires a signal, calling all connected functions associated with the Signal with the given arguments. signal:Fire("Hello, world!", 100, false)

searchDocs.title: FireAsync​
searchDocs.content: </> Signal:FireAsync(...: any) → () Fires a signal, calling all connected functions associated with the Signal with the given arguments in a new thread. signal:FireAsync("Hello, world!", 100, false)
